steps.title: Break the charge into two pieces
steps.content: The first $25$ people cost 28 dollars each, a fixed amount of $25\cdot 28=700$ dollars. Anyone beyond that is charged 17 dollars each, and there are $n-25$ such people when $n\ge 25$.

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• A) $17n+275$: gives 700 dollars at $n=25$ and adds 17 dollars per extra person. Matches.
  • B) $17n+28$: at $n=25$ this is 453 dollars, far less than 700 dollars. Eliminated.
  • C) $17n+700$: at $n=25$ this is 1,125 dollars, overcharging the base group. Eliminated.
  • D) $45n-425$: at $n=25$ this is 700 dollars (coincidence), but slope is 45 dollars per person, not 17 dollars. Eliminated.
A is correct.
